<!-- Thanks for contributing to Pillars. Fill in what's relevant; delete what isn't. -->

## What does this PR change?

<!-- One- or two-sentence summary. -->

## Kind of change

<!-- Pick one. Larger changes (structural, new pillar) should reference a prior issue with the discussion. -->

- [ ] Typo or clarification (no discussion needed)
- [ ] New worked example in `examples/` (no discussion needed)
- [ ] New pillar in the catalog (link to discussion issue: #)
- [ ] Boundary call refinement (link to discussion issue: #)
- [ ] New sub-pillar pattern (link to discussion issue: #)
- [ ] Structural change to template / schema / protocol (link to RFC issue: #)
- [ ] Documentation improvement (README, FAQ, CONTRIBUTING, etc.)
- [ ] Other (explain below)

## Why?

<!-- The motivation. If this fixes a bug or implements a discussed proposal, link the issue. -->

## Checklist

<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] Change is consistent with the "briefings, not rulebooks" philosophy
- [ ] Any new pillar follows the 8-section template
- [ ] Any new pillar declares frontmatter correctly
- [ ] Any new pillar adds tiebreaker guidance to `PILLARS.md` if it overlaps existing pillars
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` updated under `[Unreleased]` (for spec, pillar, or pattern changes)
- [ ] Examples (if any) carry the standard adopter-note header

## Anything reviewers should focus on?

<!-- Specific places that need a careful read. -->
